name: beta=0.10 finite-N robustness slice (absorption-grade)
description: Second-beta robustness slice for the Death-of-a-chimera campaign. Re-runs the absorption-grade tracer at beta=0.10 (the value the reduced model is validated against) at two corners selected by reduced-model placement (place_beta010.py): A_post=0.5 (post-homoclinic, A_hc(0.10)=0.354) and A_stable=0.2 (stable stationary-chimera band, A_SN=0.178 < 0.2 < A_H=0.278). Same canonical seed family, integrator settings, labels and tracer as absorption.config.json; only beta and the sweep design change. Rows carry campaign='beta010-v1'.

model._note: beta=0.10 only; dt/sampleStride/t_max identical to absorption.config.json so labels are computed by the same cadence.

phase_subset._note: Breath-phase locking subset: re-trace the lowest-id seeds per sweep point with R_incoh kept through absorption, so true-absorption phase phi_abs can be Rayleigh-tested. fromSweeps derives the points (A_post, A_stable, A_depth) and per-A seed0 from the sweeps above.

sweeps.label: A_depth
sweeps._note: Depth-matched contingency point (added after the A=0.5 slice came in at 2.24 breath periods, < 3). A_hc(0.10)=0.354 + operating-corner depth 0.0904 [= 0.5 - A_hc(0.05)=0.4096] = 0.444. Same distance beyond the homoclinic as the paper's operating corner, so the ratchet/aging/phase analyses are depth-matched and the 'phenomenology travels' claim is testable.
